episodes.summary: Over 52 minutes, systematically visited two local web applications (localhost:4090 jobs page and localhost:5174 dashboard with chat/episodes features) in repeating sequences, returning to the jobs page multiple times. No visible dev tools or automated testing. Ended with Discord visits, suggesting communication about the work. Pattern suggests QA verification or feature review.
episodes.methodology: Exploratory sequential browsing treating the application as a collection of discrete, testable views (jobs, chat, episodes). Manual page-by-page navigation with no automated tools or debugging. Long gaps between visits (up to 90 minutes) indicate interrupted attention—not a focused debugging session but rather a lightweight, exploratory smoke test performed in parallel with other work.

abduction.intent: Lightweight verification that the application's key pages are accessible and functional. The repeated returns to the jobs page suggest checking for consistency or confirming nothing has regressed. This appears to be either initial exploratory testing before formal QA, or a quick visual sign-off between development iterations.
abduction.mental_model: They view the application as a modular system where each view (jobs, chat, episodes) is independently testable and should be responsive. No evidence of understanding internal state management or data flow—just surface-level verification that UI loads. The application is understood as a collection of working parts, not a unified system.
abduction.confidence: exploring - methodically visiting pages without a specific bug to chase, suggesting baseline/checkpoint testing rather than targeted debugging
abduction.emotional_state: focused but flexible - rhythmic navigation suggests checking items off a list rather than investigating problems. Long gaps and context switches indicate low urgency and flexible scheduling
key_decisions: Chose manual browsing over automated tests (suggests lightweight verification, exploratory phase, or preference for visual confirmation)
Visited jobs page most frequently (reveals priority or criticality of that feature)
Ended with Discord communication (shifting to team discussion rather than solo testing)

19:35:01 - Switch to Discord (shifts from individual testing to collaborative communication)
avoidance: Browser DevTools or Inspector (would surface network errors, console logs, or state issues)
Application logs or error tracking (would require deeper investigation than page visits)

episodes.summary: Over 4 hours (interspersed, not continuous), watched YouTube and Bilibili videos on unrelated topics: ADHD/hyperactivity (searched in Chinese), cooking/egg noodles, and TouchDesigner creative tool tutorials. Performed targeted searches rather than following algorithm recommendations. Activity was secondary—interspersed with primary work—suggesting breaks, procrastination, or exploratory research.
episodes.methodology: Search-driven, ad-hoc discovery: identify topic → search → watch → search related → repeat. Low commitment to any topic (no bookmarking, note-taking, or progression through courses). Language-switching based on content (Chinese for personal interests, English for technical). Treats video as lightweight, interruptible medium—context switches back to other work between videos.

abduction.intent: Threefold: (1) Break/leisure activity during development work, (2) Exploratory technical learning about TouchDesigner (potential tool evaluation), (3) Personal research into specific topics (ADHD causes, cooking techniques). The shift to TouchDesigner at the end suggests growing focus toward work-relevant learning, but overall pattern is opportunistic rather than planned.
abduction.mental_model: Video platform understood as on-demand reference library for both hobby and work interests. They treat video as passive, guilt-free way to context-switch—no investment required. No evidence of intentional learning system (no progression, review, or reference building). Video is consumable, not knowledge resource to build on.
abduction.confidence: exploring - random-walk through unrelated topics with no coherent learning path. Only TouchDesigner section shows slight intention to deepen (clicked playlist), suggesting that topic is gaining priority
abduction.emotional_state: distracted and casual - frequent context switches, no sustained focus, no frustration. This appears to be legitimate research breaks with genuine curiosity about diverse topics, not stress-driven procrastination
key_decisions: Chose video over text-based research (faster, more passive, easier to interrupt without guilt)
Searched for Chinese-language ADHD content specifically (language preference for personal/non-technical topics)
Ended with TouchDesigner tutorial search (signal that technical learning became more intentional near end of browsing session)

episodes.summary: In a focused 3+ minute terminal session, executed a test-verify-fix-debug cycle on a JavaScript/Node project ('bisimulator'). Discussed shared function strategy (extract_episodes), ran npm test (detected 2 errors, fixed them), re-ran full test suite (all tests passed), restarted servers, launched npm run experiment in background, then modified cli.py to capture subprocess stdout/stderr to log files. Experiment failed with exit code 1; immediately pivoted to error log investigation.
episodes.methodology: Test-first checkpoint system: verify tests pass BEFORE running expensive process. Incremental code changes focused on observability (logging) rather than blind retry. Parallelizes work (lets experiment run background) while continuing investigation. Systematic: code → test → restart → experiment → monitor. When failure occurs, gathers visibility data (logs) before re-running.

abduction.intent: Primary: fix failing experiment/pipeline. Secondary: improve instrumentation and logging to prevent future blind debugging. The subprocess.run modification reveals they've been burned before by lack of visibility—this is defensive programming born from experience.
abduction.mental_model: Multi-stage pipeline architecture: code → test → server → experiment. Each checkpoint must verify before proceeding. Experiment is understood as long-running, instrumentation-heavy process. Logging is the critical lever for debugging. They recognize that better observability prevents future failures.
abduction.confidence: executing initially (running familiar, practiced steps), shifts to mixed when experiment fails and investigation begins. The failure breaks the happy path and forces exploration
abduction.emotional_state: focused and methodical - calm execution of test-restart-experiment sequence without deviation. Likely moment of frustration at exit code 1, but immediately channels it into systematic log investigation rather than panic. This suggests experience with this failure mode.
key_decisions: Run full test suite BEFORE experiment (risk mitigation: avoid wasting time on broken code)
Restart servers between test and experiment run (ensures clean state, isolation, prevents false failures)
Modify logging/instrumentation AFTER observing failure (principle: gather visibility first, then retry)
Let experiment run in background (parallelization: don't block on ~8 minute process, continue investigating)

turning_points: 23:08:44 - Decision point: 'Actually no - keep it simple. extract_episodes is the shared function' (resolved code strategy debate; committed to simpler design)
23:09:04 - 'ALL TESTS PASSED' (green light checkpoint; cleared to proceed to experiment phase)
23:10:32 - Code modification to cli.py subprocess handling (pivoted from 'run experiment' to 'improve instrumentation first'; suggests learned lesson about visibility)
23:11:36 - 'Background command failed with exit code 1' (failure detection; triggered shift from execution to investigation mode)
avoidance: Did not skip testing phase (avoided running experiment with potentially broken code)
Did not immediately re-run experiment after failure with identical code (avoided repeat failure; first improves visibility)
Did not commit code changes before testing (avoided shipping broken code)
Did not guess at root cause (went straight to investigating actual logs/output)
